user_prompt: is `src\bt_audio_demo.cpp` missing SBC encoding and decoding?
agent_reply: Yes, probably. More precisely: it is missing **explicit mSBC encode/decode** if the HFP data callbacks deliver raw SCO payload bytes rather than decoded PCM.
For HFP there are two common audio codecs:
- **CVSD narrowband**: 8 kHz voice
- **mSBC wideband**: 16 kHz voice, encoded using a constrained SBC variant

Our `bt_audio_demo.cpp` currently assumes the callback buffers are already usable as signed 16-bit mono PCM and just changes local I2S/PDM sample rate between 8 kHz and 16 kHz. That may be enough only if Bluedroid is doing codec conversion before invoking `esp_hf_client_register_data_callback()` callbacks.

But the ESP-IDF docs describe those buffers as **HCI synchronous packet payloads**, not clearly “decoded PCM.” The enum name `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_CONNECTED_MSBC` strongly suggests that when wideband is negotiated, the payload may be **mSBC frames**, not PCM. If so:
- `hfp_incoming_audio()` needs **mSBC decode** before writing to I2S.
- `hfp_outgoing_audio()` needs **mSBC encode** after reading 16 kHz PCM from PDM.
- CVSD may also not be plain PCM depending on stack behavior, though many examples treat callback data as 16-bit samples.
So the honest answer is: the current code has **wideband rate switching**, but not real mSBC handling. It is incomplete for robust HFP wideband audio.
A practical next step would be either:
1. Force/assume narrowband CVSD only and remove the `CONNECTED_MSBC` direct pipe, or
2. Add an SBC/mSBC codec path and only write/read PCM at the I2S boundary.

A practical next step would be either:

2. Add an SBC/mSBC codec path and only write/read PCM at the I2S boundary.

user_prompt: ok change the demo to use 16 kHz because I think this SBC library only works from 16 KHz and up anyways, verify this if you need to
agent_reply: Changed [src/bt_audio_demo.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp) to make the HFP audio demo 16 kHz-oriented.
What changed:
- Replaced the 8 kHz/16 kHz split with a single `kHfpDemoSampleRate = 16000`.
- mSBC/wideband now configures the local I2S/PDM path at 16 kHz.
- CVSD/narrowband no longer pipes audio directly; it logs a warning and keeps audio disabled, since that native path is 8 kHz and would need resampling or a separate CVSD path.
- Updated comments to note that `libsbc` starts at `SBC_FREQ_16000`.
Verified locally: `libsbc` supports `16000`, `32000`, `44100`, and `48000` sample-rate modes, and the PlatformIO build passes.

user_prompt: outside of this chat, ChatGPT pointed out that we must basically force this version of libsbc to ignore the header and just force 15 block mode, is that what you did?
agent_reply: Mostly, but not completely.
What I implemented: when the decoder sees the mSBC sync byte `0xAD`, it ignores the normal SBC block-mode header bits and forces:
```cpp
frame->block_mode = SBC_BLK_15;
frame->blocks = 15;
So yes: for the specific impossible part, the 15-block count, the library does not trust the header.

What I did not do: force the entire mSBC profile. The decoder still reads frequency, channel mode, allocation, subbands, and bitpool from the SBC header byte/fields. If the advice was “mSBC is fixed-format, so force 16 kHz, mono, loudness, 8 subbands, bitpool 26, 15 blocks when syncword is `0xAD`,” then we should tighten this further.
The encoder side is already effectively forced by `bt_audio_demo.cpp`:
```cpp
sbc.frequency = SBC_FREQ_16000;
sbc.mode = SBC_MODE_MONO;
sbc.allocation = SBC_AM_LOUDNESS;
sbc.subbands = SBC_SB_8;
sbc.blocks = SBC_BLK_15;
sbc.bitpool = 26;

I’d recommend we also make the decoder force those same mSBC settings whenever it sees `0xAD`. That would make the local `libsbc` behavior match “mSBC mode” more directly instead of treating it as regular SBC with one special-case block count.
Mostly, but not completely.
What I implemented: when the decoder sees the mSBC sync byte `0xAD`, it ignores the normal SBC block-mode header bits and forces:
```cpp
frame->block_mode = SBC_BLK_15;
frame->blocks = 15;
So yes: for the specific impossible part, the 15-block count, the library does not trust the header.

What I did not do: force the entire mSBC profile. The decoder still reads frequency, channel mode, allocation, subbands, and bitpool from the SBC header byte/fields. If the advice was “mSBC is fixed-format, so force 16 kHz, mono, loudness, 8 subbands, bitpool 26, 15 blocks when syncword is `0xAD`,” then we should tighten this further.
The encoder side is already effectively forced by `bt_audio_demo.cpp`:
```cpp
sbc.frequency = SBC_FREQ_16000;
sbc.mode = SBC_MODE_MONO;
sbc.allocation = SBC_AM_LOUDNESS;
sbc.subbands = SBC_SB_8;
sbc.blocks = SBC_BLK_15;
sbc.bitpool = 26;

I’d recommend we also make the decoder force those same mSBC settings whenever it sees `0xAD`. That would make the local `libsbc` behavior match “mSBC mode” more directly instead of treating it as regular SBC with one special-case block count.

user_prompt: confirm that all audio in `src\bt_audio_demo.cpp` is 16 bit PCM, and the only possible differences in streams is 8 kHZ vs 16 kHZ sample rates
agent_reply: Short version: **not exactly**.
In [src/bt_audio_demo.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp), the **local audio endpoints** are configured as 16-bit mono PCM:
- Speaker I2S: `I2S_BITS_PER_SAMPLE_16BIT` at [line 132](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:132)
- Mic PDM/I2S: `I2S_BITS_PER_SAMPLE_16BIT` at [line 166](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:166)
- Both use `I2S_CHANNEL_FMT_ONLY_RIGHT`, so mono.
- The only local sample rates selected are `8000` and `16000` at [lines 31-32](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:31).
But **not all streams in the file are 16-bit PCM**:
- In CVSD mode, the code passes HFP callback bytes directly to/from I2S: [line 267](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:267), [line 314](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:314).

- In mSBC mode, the Bluetooth-side stream is SBC/mSBC encoded. The file decodes incoming audio before I2S at [line 249](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:249), and encodes outgoing mic PCM before returning it at [line 301](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp:301).
So the precise statement is:

**The local mic/speaker audio handled by I2S/PDM is 16-bit mono PCM, and its only configured sample-rate difference is 8 kHz CVSD vs 16 kHz mSBC. The Bluetooth HFP callback stream itself is not guaranteed to be PCM; ESP-IDF describes it as HCI synchronous packet payload, and this file treats mSBC as encoded SBC data.**
ESP-IDF HFP callback docs: https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/v4.4.2/esp32/api-reference/bluetooth/esp_hf_client.html
